channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Veteran Daniel Penny, acquitted in NYC subway chokehold, will join Trump's suite at football game
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Daniel Penny, a military veteran who choked an agitated New York subway rider and was acquitted of homicide this week, has been invited by Vice President-elect JD Vance to join Donald Trump's suite at the Army-Navy football game on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Raskin demands FBI vetting of Trump nominees, details on Epshteyn
item.description: Rep. Jamie Raskin (D-Md.) on Friday made a series of demands of the Trump transition team, asking for assurances President-elect Trump’s Cabinet nominees will undergo FBI vetting while also requesting details about aide Boris Epshteyn’s alleged profiting from promoting those seeking new posts.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hill to chair House Financial Services Committee, Walberg to lead Education and Workforce panel
item.description: House Republican leaders on Thursday choose Rep. French Hill (R-Ark.) to lead the House Financial Services Committee in the 119th Congress and Rep. Tim Walberg (R-Mich.) to chair the House Committee on Education and the Workforce, completing their selections for committee chairs.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard struggles to woo senators ahead of confirmation fight
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard is struggling through her meetings with senators this week, sources told The Hill, highlighting the difficult path she faces in winning confirmation to be director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Legislators across political spectrum ‘equally angry’ over handling of drone sightings: New Jersey lawmaker
item.description: New Jersey State Assemblyman Paul Kanitra described the government's handling of the weekslong drone sightings across the northeast as “infuriating.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: O’Malley: Calling Social Security a 'legal Ponzi scheme' is bull
item.description: "Social Security is not on a path for bankruptcy or insolvency," O'Malley said during a Thursday appearance on "The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Biden administration announces $500M aid package for Ukraine
item.description: Ukraine will receive another $500 million in military assistance from the United States to help move its stalled war against Russia, the State Department said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House releases national strategy to combat Islamophobia
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House on Thursday announced what it called the first-ever national strategy to counter Islamophobia, detailing more than 100 steps federal officials are taking to curb hate, violence, bias and discrimination against Muslims and Arab Americans.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ontario to restrict electricity exports to US and bar American-made alcohol if Trump tariffs applied
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada’s most populous province may bar American-made alcohol in addition to restricting electricity exports to Michigan, New York and Minnesota if U.S. President-elect Donald Trump imposes sweeping tariffs on all Canadian products, a senior official said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: More than 20 days into phenomenon, Pentagon still has no answers about origins of mysterious NJ drones
item.description: More than three weeks after dozens of mysterious drones began popping up over New Jersey, the public has still been offered no clear insight on what the phenomenon could be.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC 'won't be a safe haven' for migrants who commit crimes, mayor says
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams on Thursday said he and incoming Trump administration border czar Tom Homan “have the same desire” to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Hill to chair House Financial Services Committee, Walberg to lead Education and Workforce panel
item.description: House Republican leaders on Thursday choose Rep. French Hill (R-Ark.) to lead the House Financial Services Committee in the 119th Congress and Rep. Tim Walberg (R-Mich.) to chair the House Committee on Education and the Workforce, completing their selections for committee chairs.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford exploring run for governor in 2026
item.description: Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford is exploring a run to become his party's nominee for governor in 2026.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Military service academies see drop in reported sexual assaults after alarming surge
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Reported sexual assaults at the U.S. military service academies dropped in 2024 for the second year in a row, according to new Pentagon data, marking a sharp turnaround from an alarming surge two years ago that triggered sweeping reviews and an overhaul in leadership.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Clinton says he’s open to talking to Biden about preemptive pardon for Hillary Clinton
item.description: “I think if President Biden wanted to talk to me about that, I will talk to him about it, but I don’t think I should be giving public advice on the pardon power,” Bill Clinton said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: RFK Jr. wants daughter-in-law in CIA top role to probe JFK assassination
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. reportedly wants Amaryllis Fox Kennedy and Trump to help release documents about the JFK assassination.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tells TIME he will pardon Jan. 6 rioters on day 1
item.description: The President-elect, who will serve his second term as the first American president to be convicted of a felony, was named TIME's 2024 Person of the Year.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is named Time's Person of the Year, will ring the New York Stock Exchange bell
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is back in his hometown of New York where he's been honored as Time magazine’s Person of the Year and will ring the opening bell at the New York Stock Exchange.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: US inflation ticked up last month as some price pressures remain persistent
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Fueled by pricier used cars, hotel rooms and groceries, inflation in the United States moved slightly higher last month in the latest sign that some price pressures remain elevated.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: DA suggests judge could permanently halt Trump’s hush money case while upholding his conviction
item.description: Prosecutors are urging a judge not to throw out President-elect Donald Trump’s hush money criminal conviction but suggesting a willingness to end the case in a way that would preserve the verdict while avoiding a protracted legal fight.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump-era Justice Department subpoenaed congressional staffers, watchdog finds
item.description: But the department's internal watchdog found no evidence of political motivation by federal prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Biden is rushing aid to Ukraine. Both sides are digging in. And everyone is bracing for Trump
item.description: The grinding war pitting Ukraine against its Russian invaders has escalated ahead of Donald Trump’s inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Florida State Rep. Susan Valdes, representing parts of Hillsborough County, switches political parties
item.description: State Rep. Susan Valdes, who represents Florida's 64th District, including parts of Hillsborough County, switched political parties, the Florida House of Representatives announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump calls for 'immediate ceasefire' in Ukraine after meeting with Zelenskyy
item.description: In a post on his Truth Social platform, Trump claimed that Moscow and Kyiv have both lost hundreds of thousands of soldiers in a war that “should never have started.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minneapolis Mayor Frey poised to block council again as his veto use continues to climb
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey has vetoed 15 actions since he first took office in 2018. That’s more vetoes than the previous two mayors combined.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Minneapolis City Council’s budget committee reduces proposed property tax levy for 2025
item.description: Council members, citing concerns about elevated costs for constituents, approved a series of amendments to the mayor’s proposed budget which would reduce the levy hike by 1.4 percent.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News
